rules.description: Capture RingCentral content's intended meaning and transfer it into businesslike and clear language suited to a wide Polish-speaking audience
rules.notes: Avoid literal translations

rules.description: Translations should be well-formed grammatically, contain correct spellings, adhere to common use of terms, titles, and names, and be intuitively acceptable
rules.notes: A native speaker should at all times be able to sensibly interpret the translations

rules.description: If topics in this style guide conflict with RingCentral approved terminology lists, the client's specific instructions take precedence
Buttons should typically be translated as verbs, generally in infinitive form
rules.notes: Be concise, avoid using articles or prepositions

rules.description: Translations of buttons do not end in a period unless they consist of more than one sentence
Capitalize the first word of the button
Do NOT use any quotation marks around buttons even if the English source uses them
rules.example_correct: Aby rozpocząć, kliknij przycisk „Przegląd i przeniesienie
rules.example_incorrect: Kliknij, aby rozpocząć, przycisk „Przegląd i przeniesienie

rules.description: Often the word 'button' itself (knop) can be left out in Polish
Product/application names are often trademarked and not to be localized
rules.notes: Check reference material and linguistic instructions, including terminology lists before translating

rules.description: Replace English examples with Polish equivalents when needed
rules.notes: Adapt examples like wildcards to Polish context

rules.description: Target audience are businesses so use formal addressing: Pan(i)/Pana(-i)
Register should be professional and welcoming, business casual, precise, clear, consistent, and natural
rules.notes: Rephrase unclear sentences, rework sentences that sound like a translation

rules.description: Always use 'masz' NOT 'mieć' for the verb 'have'
For product UI, Support Content and Customer Training: be accurate, concise, clear, coherent, objective, correct, complete
rules.notes: No ambiguities, mistranslations, colloquial or slang words

rules.description: Avoid condescending, childish or overly familiar tone; treat reader as adult professional
rules.notes: Downplay humor, keep tone business-like

rules.description: For Marketing Content, emails, Product Guides, PR letters: use idiomatically correct language that doesn't sound like translation
rules.notes: Use correct verbs, metaphors, expressions

rules.description: Avoid literal translations; balance faithfulness to source with readability in target language
Avoid passive voice and use of word 'men'; use active form to improve readability
rules.notes: Passive voice only for legal/official documents like privacy policies, contracts, NDAs

rules.description: Avoid use of second-person possessive 'Twoje akta'; simple articles can replace possessive adjectives
Cross-references must be referenced consistently throughout translation and all files
rules.notes: Check if documents have been localized before and match relevant translations

rules.description: For external cross-references to untranslated documents, leave in source language
rules.notes: Contact PM or Language Lead if unable to retrieve translation information

rules.description: Be more direct than English; use imperative form instead of over-polite constructions with 'Please'
Try to use gender-neutral writing as much as possible; find balance between bias-free language and fluency
rules.example_correct: Zespół obsługi skontaktuje się z klientem i poinformuje go.
rules.example_incorrect: Zespół obsługi skontaktuje się z klientem i poinformuje go/ją.

rules.description: Avoid overly long or complex compounds; unintuitive compounds are usability issue
rules.notes: For product UI, Support Content and Customer Training Materials

rules.description: Avoid overtranslation - use of unnecessary words where less verbose version would be equally clear
Avoid literal translations that don't follow Polish style and idiom rules
rules.example_correct: Napisanie złożonych wzorów może być łatwiejsze, jeśli ich części zawierają nazwane, krótsze wzory.
rules.example_incorrect: Możesz też stwierdzić, że łatwiej jest napisanie złożonych formuł, jeśli części tych formuł zawieraja nazwane, krótsze formuły.
rules.notes: Can result in hard to understand or ungrammatical sentences

rules.section: Terminology and Word Choice - Technical Terms
rules.description: RingCentral Glossary is first and binding source for technical terms
rules.notes: For unlisted terms, use Microsoft standard terminology, online resources like Wikipedia, IT vendor portals

rules.description: Follow Polish capitalization rules, not English ones
When referring to UI elements, always use Uppercase
First word following colon should not be capitalized unless there is more than one sentence after colon
rules.notes: If more than one sentence, first word should be capitalized

rules.description: In sentences and headings, only use uppercase at beginning of sentence
Even if titles and headings are fully capitalized in English, follow Polish rules
rules.notes: Exception: legal texts where capitalization conveys specific meaning

rules.description: Product names follow the source capitalization
Use lowercase in months, days, and currencies
Run spell-checker when translation is complete; use Word grammar checker if possible
Brand name tags need to be separated from preceding or following text with a space
Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces in Polish
rules.notes: English documents often include double spaces after full stops

rules.description: Use non-breaking spaces to prevent separation of names, numbers, software names and versions at line end
rules.notes: Ctrl + Shift + Space bar in MS Word or Alt+0160; do not use in software files

rules.description: Follow Polish punctuation rules as described at https://jzyk-polski.pl/zasady-interpunkcji
Use comma where sentence would be too long or complicated, or where omission could cause confusion
rules.notes: Do not use comma to separate two sentences

rules.description: Period is used to end sentence, paragraph, or abbreviated word
Acronyms and units of measurement are not spelled with periods
Do not use full stops after headings, titles, table columns, or captions unless expression is full sentence
Do not use full stops if sentence ends with abbreviation, interrogation/exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon
Try to avoid using colons, especially in marketing related content
Use colons to introduce lists and at end of sentence that introduces a procedure
If colon is followed by single sentence, first word after colon does not get capital
Semicolons are preferably avoided
rules.notes: If used, must follow Polish punctuation rules - complete sentence with personal form must follow

rules.description: For marketing and PR content semicolons should never be used
rules.notes: Cut sentence into two smaller ones to enhance readability

rules.description: When ellipsis dots are used in English, they must be used in Polish as well
Use normal hyphen (-) to connect components of compounds, separate words across line breaks, as ellipsis marker, and as minus sign
Use round brackets for explanations that would disrupt sentence and when adding full form of acronym
rules.notes: First letter is lowercase unless sentence in parentheses is independent

rules.description: Area code of phone numbers is written between brackets
Use square brackets for indicating variables or additional information within round brackets
rules.notes: Follow guidelines at https://sjp.pwn.pl/zasady/Pisownia-skrotow-i-skrotowcow;629564.html

rules.description: NEVER use straight single quote for variables
rules.notes: Updated on 20241207

rules.description: Double quotes are used for direct quotations
Use the same format in translation as in original text for enumerations
Sentence introducing enumeration ends with colon; each part starts with capital and ends with period
rules.example_correct:
Możliwe są następujące przypadki:
● Serwer znajduje się w sieci.
● Serwer znajduje się poza siecią.

rules.description: If enumeration parts consist of single word or small group of words, punctuation marks may be omitted
rules.example_correct:
Serwer może być zlokalizowany w następujących miejscach:
● w obrębie sieci
● poza siecią

rules.description: If enumeration consists of phrases/sentence parts: introduction ends with colon, each part starts lowercase and ends with semicolon, last part gets period
Do not use abbreviations in body text; write expressions like 'np.' and 'dot.' in full
rules.notes: Abbreviations only when absolutely necessary, for units or space issues

rules.description: Be consistent - do not abbreviate term in some sentences and spell out in others
In on-screen text, abbreviations only allowed in exceptional situations; remove articles and prepositions first if space insufficient
rules.notes: Do not use telegram style if not necessary

rules.description: Follow capitalization of abbreviated words; if organization uses different notation, follow their notation
rules.notes: Often applies to product names and standards

rules.description: For acronyms without capitals, hyphen/apostrophe disappears in compounds; for acronyms with capitals, retain capital and apostrophe
When acronym appears first time, spell it out with full term in brackets after acronym
rules.notes: After initial clarification, acronym can be used alone

rules.description: If acronym refers to untranslated term, give full English term; if term has accepted Polish translation, use acronym followed by Polish term
These acronyms require no explanation: ADSL MS-DOS GUI pc ASCII ANSI EGA VGA PCI ROM UNIX TCP/IP cd dvd
Numbers should be written in full when used as nouns, appearing alone representing simple quantities (usually up to 12), and in expressions like 'in the 80s'
Dates, large numbers, ages, document references should be written as numbers
Replace decimal point with comma and thousand separator with space
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms
Poland uses metric system by law; avoid pounds, miles, inches, feet, gallons
rules.notes: Monitor sizes are exception - always expressed in inches

rules.description: For imperial units, add converted metric value first and leave original in brackets, or convert completely based on project requirements
Units of measure should be separated from preceding number by non-breaking space
After abbreviations of units of measurement there is no period according to official Polish standards
Percent sign is % without leading space
Default Short Date Format is d-m-yy (day without leading zero, month without leading zero, year without century)
Default Long Date Format is dddd mmmm yyyy with day names optional, month spelled out, year in four digits, lowercase for days and months
rules.example_correct: wtorek 26 maja 2020 r. OR 26 maja 2020 roku

rules.description: In general context, use fully written form in 24-hour clock format
rules.example_correct: Zapisy rozpoczynaja się o godzinie 8:30. Godziny pracy: 9:30 – 12:30; 13:30-18:30.

rules.description: In technical and scientific texts, symbols h, min, and s are common, written between figures
rules.notes: These symbols are internationally agreed ISO standard

rules.description: Currency amounts can be written as '23,50 euro' or '23,50 EUR'; second variant with EUR abbreviation is preferred
rules.notes: Also: 50 centów

rules.description: Pay attention to correct use of prepositions; consult Microsoft Language Portal for UI content
rules.notes: Influenced by English, many linguists omit them or change word order

rules.description: Application/product names are often trademarked and rarely translated; verify translatability before translating
rules.notes: Check reference material and linguistic instructions

rules.description: Use trademark symbols only on first mention in translated content unless otherwise indicated
rules.notes: Refer to most updated RingCentral term list for Polish

rules.description: For third party trademarks, research to determine if official approved trademarked translated version is available
rules.notes: Follow localized equivalence on official websites if available

rules.description: Use official approved Polish translation of copyright notices when available
rules.example_correct: ©2015 RingCentral, Inc. Wszystkie prawa zastrzeżone. RingCentral, RingCentral Office, RingCentral Meetings i logo RingCentral są zastrzeżonymi znakami firmowymi RingCentral, Inc.
rules.notes: If no official translation available, use provided Polish version

rules.description: Query if URLs have local versions and modify document accordingly; if not, leave as source English text
If software has been translated, match relevant translation memory or string list
If software has not been translated, use bilingual format with English first, Polish in brackets outside formatting
When Microsoft terminology or UI items are quoted, refer to Microsoft approved terminology on MS Portal
rules.notes: http://www.microsoft.com/Language/en-US/Default.aspx

rules.description: Translate country and city names based on Polish conventions; localize address formats if necessary
If source only includes toll free and US numbers, add international country code for international users
